'''Esene Faimalo''' (born 11 October 1966) is a  former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s, who represented both [[Samoa national rugby league team|Western Samoa]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at international level.

==Playing career==
In 1985 Faimalo came second in the New Zealand boxing Heavyweight tournament and also made the [[Junior Kiwis]] while playing for the Linwood club.<ref name=boxing /><ref name=cxiii>Coffey, John. ''Canterbury XIII'', Christchurch, 1987.</ref> Faimalo represented [[Western Samoa national rugby league team|Western Samoa]] at the [[1986 Pacific Cup]].<ref name=cxiii/>

A [[Canterbury rugby league team|Canterbury representative]] from the Addington club,<ref name=p133>''1986 Lion Red Rugby League Annual'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1986. p.133</ref> Faimalo also represented the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] between 1988 and 1991, playing in five test matches. Faimalo spent 1989 with the [[Balmain Tigers]] as part of the [[New Zealand Rugby League]]'s "Rookie Scheme".<ref>Lion Red 1989 Rugby League Annual ''New Zealand Rugby League'', p.10</ref>

In 1990 he moved to England, signing with the [[Widnes Vikings]]. He played for the club for four seasons, making 111 appearances and scoring 11 tries,<ref name="rugby.widnes.tv bio">{{cite web|title=Esene Faimalo|url=http://www.rugby.widnes.tv/playerbiog.php?mid=222|work=Widnes RLFC|access-date=18 February 2014}}</ref>  before signing with the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in 1994, where he scored seven tries in 54 appearances.<ref>{{cite web|title=Esene Faimalo|url=http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84016&includeref=dynamic|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|access-date=18 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140222170955/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84016&includeref=dynamic|archive-date=22 February 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> He joined the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] during 1996's  [[Super League I]] season, playing with the club for three years.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20121105054515/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-4830794.html Rugby League] ''The Independent'', 5 October 1996</ref> He returned to Widnes in 2000, making a further five appearances before retiring.<ref name="rugby.widnes.tv bio" /><ref name="rugby.widnes.tv">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugby.widnes.tv/search.php?q=Faimalo|title=Statistics at rugby.widnes.tv|publisher=rugby.widnes.tv|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref>

In 2010 Faimalo entered the boxing ring in a charity bout to raise money for the Steve Prescott Foundation.<ref name=boxing>[http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/8715660.Scully_slugs_it_out_with_Brian/?ref=rss Scully slugs it out with Brian] ''St Helens Star'', 2 December 2010</ref>

==Personal life==
Esene Faimalo is the brother of the rugby league footballer [[Joe Faimalo]].<ref name="rugby.widnes.tv" />
